It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Cultural Properties of Nankoku === * Historic Sites ** Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group - Designated on April 30, 1966 (Shōwa 41). <ref>“Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group,” *Nankoku City History*, Volume 2, Nankoku City, 1982, pp. 1020-1021.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.570386|N|133.616022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22120567]] Wikidata name: Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group''' (明見彦山古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], in the Myōken area. It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi]], Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>

It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi}}, Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.570386|N|133.616022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22120567]] Wikidata name: Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group''' (明見彦山古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|WD=Q861621|lt=Nankoku}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}, in the Myōken area. It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi}}, Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Cultural Properties of Nankoku === * Historic Sites ** Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group - Designated on April 30, 1966 (Shōwa 41). <ref>“Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group,” *Nankoku City History*, Volume 2, Nankoku City, 1982, pp. 1020-1021.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Historical site explanation board (installed by Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group|title=Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|series=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 40|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report No. 3|publisher=Kōchi University Faculty of Humanities Archaeological Research Laboratory|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006}}}} - Link from the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties’ "Comprehensive National Archaeological Report Database." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Nankoku: Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/maibun/maibunkochi25.pdf Maibun Kōchi]}}|number=25|publisher=Kōchi Prefecture Board of Education Cultural Properties Division|isbn=|pages=7|ref={{Harvid|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012}}}}

It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi}}, Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Cultural Properties of Nankoku === * Historic Sites ** Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group - Designated on April 30, 1966 (Shōwa 41). <ref>“Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group,” *Nankoku City History*, Volume 2, Nankoku City, 1982, pp. 1020-1021.</ref>

Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Cultural Properties of Nankoku === * Historic Sites ** Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group - Designated on April 30, 1966 (Shōwa 41). <ref>“Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group,” *Nankoku City History*, Volume 2, Nankoku City, 1982, pp. 1020-1021.</ref>

== References == * Historical site explanation board (installed by Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group|title=Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|series=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 40|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report No. 3|publisher=Kōchi University Faculty of Humanities Archaeological Research Laboratory|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006}}}} - Link from the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties’ "Comprehensive National Archaeological Report Database." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Nankoku: Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/maibun/maibunkochi25.pdf Maibun Kōchi]}}|number=25|publisher=Kōchi Prefecture Board of Education Cultural Properties Division|isbn=|pages=7|ref={{Harvid|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012}}}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.570386|N|133.616022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group''' (明見彦山古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|WD=Q861621|lt=Nankoku}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}, in the Myōken area. It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi}}, Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Cultural Properties of Nankoku === * Historic Sites ** Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group - Designated on April 30, 1966 (Shōwa 41). <ref>“Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group,” *Nankoku City History*, Volume 2, Nankoku City, 1982, pp. 1020-1021.</ref>

It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>

<!--Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster--><!--Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q22120567}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nangoku]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.570386|N|133.616022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group''' (明見彦山古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|WD=Q861621|lt=Nankoku|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, in the Myōken area. It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>

The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group]][[ja:明見彦山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.570386|N|133.616022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group''' (明見彦山古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|WD=Q861621|lt=Nankoku|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, in the Myōken area. It is designated as a municipal historic site by Nankoku City.
Situated at the foot of a hill referred to as "Hikoyama," this kofun group comprises three Kofuns from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}: No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 kofun. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
== List == === Kofun No. 1 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 1-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 1}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 1}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1''' ({{Coord|33|34|13.39|N|133|36|57.68|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 1}}) is located to the southwest of the group and is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa," along with the {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, Asakura) and the {{ill|Koren Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koren Kofun|qq=}} (in Nankoku, Okoyachō Koren). Recent surveys include a mound measurement and stone chamber survey conducted in 2005 (Heisei 17) {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}, and an excavation survey in 2010 (Heisei 22). {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Cultural Properties of Nankoku === * Historic Sites ** Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group - Designated on April 30, 1966 (Shōwa 41). <ref>“Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group,” *Nankoku City History*, Volume 2, Nankoku City, 1982, pp. 1020-1021.</ref>

The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>

The mound is believed to be a round tumulus with a diameter of 14 meters (possibly a square tumulus with sides of 14 meters). It features a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double-sleeve construction. {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=5-11, 23-29}}
The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows: {{Sfn|Survey of Large Late Kofun in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 5-11, 23-29}}{{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} * Total chamber length: 8.96 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.64 meters, width 2.16 meters (rear wall), width 2.14 meters (entrance), height 2.8 meters * Entrance passage: Length 3.32 meters, width 1.20 meters
The chamber has been plundered, but remains of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and jewelry have been discovered. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}} The tumulus is estimated to date back to the late 6th to early 7th century. {{Sfn|Maibun Kōchi No. 25|2012|p=7}}
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the opening) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (towards the main chamber) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-1, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
=== Kofun No. 2 === '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 2''' was located east of No. 1 kofun. However, it has since been destroyed and no details remain. It is said to have featured a larger stone chamber than No. 3 kofun, indicating its function as a leader’s tomb. {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}}
=== Kofun No. 3 === [[File:Myokenhikoyama-kofun 3-1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the stone chamber of Kofun No. 3}}]] [[File:Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber in Kofun No. 3}}]] '''Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3''' ({{Coord|33|34|17.81|N|133|37|1.18|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=Myōkenhikoyama Kofun No. 3}}) is located approximately 200 meters northeast of No. 1 kofun. It is a round tumulus with a diameter of 5 meters and a height of 2.3 meters, featuring a double-sleeve {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekkaku|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. <ref name="Explanation Board">Historical site explanation board.</ref>
The dimensions of the stone chamber are: {{Sfn|Myōkenhikoyama Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1983}} * Total chamber length: 5.8 meters * Entrance passage length: 1.8 meters
Artifacts such as human bones, Sue pottery, straight swords, daggers, horse fittings, iron sickles, iron plow blades, magatama, and tubular beads were unearthed from within the chamber. <ref name="Explanation Board"/> The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. <ref name="Explanation Board"/>
<gallery> Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, 3D-N.jpg|Stone chamber layout Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) </gallery>
